Full Question Text: Does your child say 4 meaningful words (not including "mum"/"dad")?
When looking at pictures in a book, does your child touch, point or try to pick up the images?
When you ask your child to find a familiar object, will they go into another room for it?
Does your child tell you they want something by pointing to the item?
Does your child say 8 or more meaningful words (not including "mum"/"dad")?
Does your child copy phrases that have two words (e.g. mum play, more bubble)?
During picture/book share, when you say "Show me the tree" or "Where's the cow?", does your child point to the correct picture?
Does your child say short phrases that represent different ideas together (e.g. "cat down," "more mum", "daddy gone")?
Can your child carry out at least three instructions WITHOUT gestural prompts? ("Get your jumper", "Open the door", etc.)
Does your child correctly name at least one picture when you point and say "what's this"?
When you ask your child to point to body parts, do they correctly point to at least seven?
Does your child use at least 2 pronoun words ("you, mine, I, me")?
Does your child say 15 or more meaningful words?
Does your child correctly name at least one picture when you point and say "what's this"?
During picture/book share, does your child point to the correct picture when asked?

Can your child talk in sentences with 3-4 words? (e.g. "big car down", "give drink to teddy")
Can your child point to at least seven body parts?
When looking at pictures in a book, does your child label or talk about actions happening? (e.g. "jumping, running, crying, hugging")
Give your child a verbal instruction such as "put the sock in the box" -- can they follow through correctly WITHOUT gestural help?
Can your child tell you their first name or nickname if you ask "what's your name?"
Does your child understand "up" and "down"?
Can your child tell you their first and last name if you ask "what's your name?"

Can your child follow instructions that have three steps, without repeating it, nor any support from gestures/pointing? For example, "Get the ball, sit down and touch your nose" OR "clap your hands, pat your head and then sit down".

Can your child follow instructions that have three steps?
When your child says sentences, do they include all the little words to make it grammatically correct?

Without your giving help by pointing or repeating, does your child follow three directions that are unrelated to one another? Give all three directions before your child starts. For example, you may ask your child, "Clap your hands, walk to the door, and sit down," or "Give me the pen, open the book, and stand up."

Does your child name at least three items from a common category? For example, if you say to your child, "Tell me some things that you can eat," does your child answer with something like "cookies, eggs, and cereal"? Or if you say, "Tell me the names of some animals," does your child answer with something like "cow, dog, and elephant"?
When your child talks to you, do they mostly put two or more words together? (e.g. "more milk", "daddy come", "mum sit" "red car")
